p.strong: GFP_KERNEL vs GFP_ATOMIC: Choose Based on Execution Context

div.p: Why: Scheduler cannot be invoked. Sleeping is physically impossible. GFP_KERNEL here → immediate BUG()

ul.li: May call schedule() — puts caller to sleep
Draws from full memory pool Success rate: Highest Latency: Variable (µs – ms under pressure) Valid contexts: Syscall handlers, work_struct callbacks, kernel threads, module_init
Draws from pre-reserved emergency memory pool
Returns NULL when pool is exhausted
No page reclaim, no waiting Success rate: Lower under memory pressure Latency: Deterministic (nanoseconds) Valid contexts: IRQ handlers, softirqs, tasklets, spinlock-held regions

g.text: return -ENOMEM; /* propagate up — do NOT crash */

div.p: ⚠️ PITFALL: GFP_KERNEL in atomic context Kernel detects this and calls BUG():
BUG: sleeping function called from invalid context at mm/slab.c:... in_atomic(): 1, irqs_disabled(): 0

ul.li: Other drivers / kernel subsystems fail to allocate

li.strong: Not conservative — it is wasteful and harmful

ul.li: NO → GFP_ATOMIC GFP flags describe your context, not the memory.
